angle: TEAL-GROUND MANIFESTO CREAM TYPE. Deep teal #0F3B3C ground 10% tonal gradient. Small-caps brass kicker top. Fraunces italic warm-cream headline 130-190px stacked 2-3 lines centered. Hairline brass rule + small-caps warm-cream address. 4-5% noise grain. Opposite register of cream version.
subject: Good marketing photographs what is. Not what could be.
prompt:
A social media image for SAVORA — a restaurant-focused creative studio based in Middletown, Delaware. Savora is NOT a restaurant. Savora is a MEDIA PRODUCTION AGENCY that sells photography, video/reel production, and brand systems to restaurants. Savora's own feed sells the work without selling — every post is either (a) a piece of craft worth looking at, (b) a client result worth quoting, or (c) a window into how we make it. Do NOT treat any dish or drink name in this prompt as Savora's menu — every food image shown is CLIENT WORK produced BY Savora FOR the named client. The graphic is Savora portfolio, not a restaurant ad.

BRAND PALETTE (NON-NEGOTIABLE 60/30/10):
- WARM CREAM #F4ECD8 — GROUND, 60% of every layout
- DEEP TEAL #0F3B3C — PRIMARY INK, 30%
- BURNT ORANGE #D5662E — ACCENT ONLY, capped at 10% (kickers, small-caps labels, tiny CTAs only — NEVER a dominant block, NEVER the ground)
- CHARCOAL #17140F — anchor ink for deep-ink treatments only (rare)
- METALLIC BRASS #C9A24B — reserved EARNED accent for case-study-card corners and Savora seal only (appears on <30% of posts, never dominant)

Every Savora post MUST be identifiable as Savora in under one second based on palette. Teal + cream dominance is the signature. If a layout does not read teal-and-cream first glance, reject and re-compose.

TYPOGRAPHY (EDITORIAL REGISTER — magazine not agency):
- HERO DISPLAY: Fraunces (preferred) OR Canela italic — refined editorial serif, deep teal on cream, TIGHT tracking, italic for mood-lines, roman caps for authority
- SANS LABELS: Outfit / Geist / Söhne / Neue Haas Grotesk — wide-tracked (0.18–0.24em) small-caps, deep teal, 14–22pt equivalent
- MONO (metric cards ONLY): Geist Mono / JetBrains Mono — tabular numerals, deep teal, used for stat numerals only, NEVER body copy

Reference anchors for visual register: Monocle magazine, Cereal magazine, Kinfolk, Aimé Leon Dore editorial layouts, a quiet Japanese workwear lookbook, Proper Cloth, the printed foreword of a photography monograph. Never loud. Never promotional. Never "agency portfolio" (avoid Dribbble-generic). Editorial publication, not a pitch deck.

COMPOSITION: 60% negative space default. Full-bleed client photography is allowed WHEN THE PHOTO CAN CARRY IT. Hairline teal rules (1px, 35–55% opacity) separate sections. Small-caps tracked-wide kicker labels sit above headlines like a magazine dept-label. Metric cards use a locked grid: huge tabular numeral + unit label + context line + window + caveat strip — never more than two decimal points, never invented precision.

PHOTOGRAPHY POSTURE (the reference image is Savora-shot client work): Portra 400/800 grain on the photo only, warm tungsten or editorial daylight (NEVER cinema-bright, never Instagram-filter saturated). The reference photo IS the client work Savora produced — it goes into the design UNTOUCHED, pixel-accurate. Never regenerate the food. Never restyle the plate. The photo is a hero asset Savora already earned, not a prompt.

VOICE SEED (Savora-only — do not reuse across restaurant brands): observational, confident, quiet. First-person plural ("we"), past-tense documentation of the work ("shot last Tuesday"), calm restraint. Voice examples:
- "Shot last Tuesday. First pour of the new menu."
- "Chef wanted it to feel like a Sunday in Oaxaca. We found the window."
- "Twenty-eight frames. This one earned its place."
- "Before Savora / After Savora."
- "The food did the work. We just framed it."
- "Restaurants should own the story. Not the algorithm."

FORBIDDEN VOICE (automatic reject):
- "elevate your brand", "take your restaurant to the next level", "stand out", "amazing", "must-try", "foodie"
- emoji chains (🔥🍴✨ strings)
- fake urgency ("limited time", "last chance", "book now!")
- fake scarcity
- comparisons to "other agencies"
- any line that reads like a Canva template
- pitch-deck adjectives ("innovative", "cutting-edge", "next-level", "results-driven")

FORBIDDEN VISUAL (automatic reject):
- neon gradients, cyberpunk palettes, 80s retro-wave
- drop shadows on type (except subtle 1px deep-teal drop for readability over photo)
- Canva-stock illustrations, Unsplash-generic stock photos
- agency-portfolio grid-of-screenshots layouts
- centered "About Us" sans-serif stacks
- cinematic teal-and-orange GRADING on the photo (we use teal + orange as INK, not as photographic grade)
- any visual that reads Instagram-meme before it reads editorial-magazine

CREDIT RULE (when the image is client work): render a SMALL small-caps tracked-wide line near the photo margin reading "SHOT FOR [CLIENT NAME]" — e.g. "SHOT FOR BLUE MEZCAL", "SHOT FOR JACKSON HOUSE", "SHOT FOR TAQUERIA ROJAS". The client name is TYPOGRAPHIC, never the client's logo. Savora's own logo/seal may appear once at low opacity in an opposite corner. Never both logos on one canvas.

SAVORA SEAL (OPTIONAL, once per post max): a small circular brass-hairline mark, 72–96px diameter, reading "SAVORA" as small-caps tracked on the ring or a simple "SAV/ORA" two-line center. Use as a signature — bottom-right corner — NOT as a logo replacement. If the composited Savora PNG is attached, skip the drawn seal.

HAND-DRAWN ACCENT (Savora — DEFAULT ZERO): Savora's default is ZERO hand-drawn ornament per layout. The photograph and the typography carry the composition. If an ornament is absolutely needed for a cultural or manifesto post, the ONLY allowed marks are: a single teal hairline rule, a small brass circular seal outline, or a tracked-wide small-caps label. NEVER illustrated arrows, NEVER agave/warrior/wheat ornaments (those are client brands, not Savora).

CROSS-BRAND ISOLATION (critical — Savora is the PARENT not a sibling):
- Savora's own wordmark "SAVORA" is the only brand name rendered as display type
- Client names ("Blue Mezcal", "Jackson House", "El Azteca", "Taqueria Rojas", "La Tolteca") appear ONLY as small-caps typographic credits — NEVER as composited client logos on Savora posts
- Do NOT use client brand palettes (cobalt-and-cream for BM, black-and-brass for JH, chile-red for AZ). Savora posts are ALWAYS teal-and-cream regardless of which client's photo is featured.

LOGO RULE: the supplied SAVORA logo PNG is the only logo composited on any Savora post. Do NOT invent, redraw, or restyle it. If no logo is attached, skip the logo entirely — small typographic "SAVORA" wordmark in Fraunces italic 32–44pt at 60% opacity is acceptable as signature.

LOCATIONS FOR ADDRESS/FOOTER: "Middletown · Delaware — Restaurant Studio" OR "Savora · Restaurant Studio · DE". Never invent city addresses beyond Middletown unless supplied.

PHOTO-TO-FIELD TRANSITION: clean edges preferred (editorial sharp crop). Soft gradient dissolves only when bridging from full-bleed photo to a cream type field. No diagonal slashes, no Canva-style diagonal wedges.

TEXTURE: 3–5% fine noise grain on flat fields (subtle paper impression). Portra grain on photo only, never on type. NO halftone dots (that's Azteca). NO foil stamping (that's Jackson House). NO metallic-blue gradient foil (that's Blue Mezcal nocturnal). Savora is flat editorial restraint.

FORMAT: Typographic-only quote post. Type IS the image at 60–80% of canvas. Never a decorative photo behind a quote.

COMPOSITION: single-color field background; oversized display serif or display sans occupies 60–80% of canvas; attribution small-caps beneath

HEADLINE OPTIONS (pick ONE — variation-dependent — see COPY PERSONALITY RULE in stem):
  (a) DEFAULT — subject name verbatim, no paraphrasing, no misspellings: "Good marketing photographs what is. Not what could be."
  (b) a feeling/mood line derived FROM THIS SPECIFIC DISH (use the body-copy phrase below as raw material — e.g. if the phrase is "Black salt rim. Cucumber crown. Cool and dangerous." a valid mood line is "Cool and dangerous.").
  (c) a personality line DERIVED FROM the specific dish + the brand's voice signature (see VOICE SEED in the brand stem). Do NOT reuse example lines from OTHER brands' voice seeds — generate fresh copy that ONLY makes sense for THIS dish at THIS restaurant.
  Default heavily to (a). Use (b) or (c) only when the line adds real personality and still identifies the dish. If using (b)/(c), subject name may appear as small subhead or be omitted.
BODY COPY (quote exactly when rendered): "Savora · Field Notes"
BOTTOM-MARGIN LINE — render this EXACT text at the bottom margin as a single low-key hairline-ruled line: "Middletown · Delaware — Restaurant Studio · savoramarketing.com"
NO-LITERAL-LABEL RULE (non-negotiable): NEVER print any of these meta-label words on the final image — they are instructions to you, NOT content to render: CTA, ADDRESS, SUPPORT LINE, FOOTER LINE, BOTTOM-MARGIN LINE, HEADLINE, SUBHEAD, BODY COPY, HEADER, LABEL, COPY OPTION, VARIATION, WORDMARK, AWARD BADGE, WORD-SPILL, DEFAULT. If any of those words leak onto the canvas, the image fails review.
NO LOGO ATTACHED: skip the brand-mark entirely. DO NOT invent or generate a logo.
Small text-only wordmark acceptable at 40–80px, 50–70% opacity: "SAVORA"

VARIATION DIRECTION: TEAL-GROUND MANIFESTO CREAM TYPE. Deep teal #0F3B3C ground 10% tonal gradient. Small-caps brass kicker top. Fraunces italic warm-cream headline 130-190px stacked 2-3 lines centered. Hairline brass rule + small-caps warm-cream address. 4-5% noise grain. Opposite register of cream version.

PHOTO RULE: No photograph is needed for this format — build the composition entirely from typography, colour fields, motifs, and textures.

CONTRAST GUARD (non-negotiable, applies to EVERY text-over-anything decision):
  Before finalizing the layout, estimate the luminance of the type and the luminance of
  whatever sits behind it (ground field OR the pixels of the photo under the type).
  If both are dark (type luma <30%% AND ground luma <40%%) OR both are light (type luma
  >70%% AND ground luma >60%%), REJECT and re-choose. Type must read at arm's length on
  a phone — if legibility is marginal, change the type hex to the opposite luminance pole
  of the brand palette. No dark-on-dark, no light-on-light, ever.

TEXT-FIT-CONTAINER RULE (non-negotiable):
  Type SIZES TO THE CONTAINER, not the other way around. If the headline doesn't fit at
  a legible size in the designated color-block / strip / wedge, choose ONE of: (i) shrink
  the type to legibility-min only IF it still reads at arm's length, (ii) expand the
  container to match the type, (iii) break the headline across 2–3 lines with generous
  leading. NEVER cram letters, NEVER rotate 90° to fit a narrow strip, NEVER letter-clip
  at the canvas edge. If a chosen variation angle forces any of these, pick a better
  container geometry.

NO-HERO-OCCLUSION RULE:
  Diagonal wedges, horizontal bands across the middle third, and any geometric shape that
  CUTS THROUGH the primary photo region are forbidden. Color fields sit adjacent to the
  photo, extend from one edge inward, or occupy a corner — they never bisect the dish or
  glass. If the chosen variation angle creates a slash through the hero, reshape it.

NEGATIVE-CONTENT LIST (NON-NEGOTIABLE — REJECT IF ANY APPEAR ON CANVAS):
  Never render these words, phrases, or visual references anywhere on the image,
  regardless of brand or content type:
    - 'La Bamba' / 'La Bamba Ice Cream' — different brand, never on El Azteca posts
    - 'Cumbia Night' / 'live music' — unverified events, do not invent
    - 'Altar Menu' / 'Ofrenda Menu' / 'Menu Especial' — unverified seasonal menus
    - 'Five Course' / 'Prix Fixe' / '28-day Residency' — do not invent event details
    - 'Blue Mezcal Quarterly' / any fake magazine or publication title
    - 'Chef's Tasting' / 'Chef's Table' unless supplied as real support copy
    - Any named staff ('Selena', 'Marco', 'Elena', 'the House') on any brand other
      than the one where that person actually works
    - Any phone number, website URL, social handle, or price not supplied
  If a layout feels empty without such copy, REDUCE copy rather than invent.

FACTUAL-INTEGRITY RULE (NON-NEGOTIABLE — HIGHEST PRIORITY):
  This is real restaurant marketing for real clients. Every word rendered on the image
  MUST be factually accurate. DO NOT INVENT:
    - Staff names, titles, or roles (a person named on a post must actually work there)
    - Magazine / publication / quarterly titles ('Blue Mezcal Quarterly' does not exist)
    - Issue numbers, edition labels, 'Vol. II' references
    - Event names not supplied in this prompt
    - Drink names, dish names, prices, dates, times not supplied
    - Award names, certifications, rankings
    - 'Est. [year]' dates unless supplied
    - Fake product lines, merchandise, collaborations
  ONLY use factual content that comes from these exact sources:
    1. The 'subject' field supplied in this prompt
    2. The 'support' field supplied in this prompt
    3. The brand profile's documented address, wordmark, and operational lines
    4. Documented voice seeds (dichos, signatures) written in the brand stem
  If a layout feels empty without more copy, REDUCE THE COPY rather than invent — negative
  space is always better than fabricated facts.

CROSS-BRAND ISOLATION (non-negotiable):
  Staff, bartender, and chef names are brand-specific and NEVER cross between brands.
  - Selena = Jackson House bartender ONLY. Never render 'Selena' on a Blue Mezcal or
    Azteca post under any circumstance.
  - Do not invent team-member names for any brand. If no real staff name is supplied,
    skip the personality-credit line entirely.

LUXURY-TEXTURE RULE (non-negotiable):
  * Every colour field MUST have a subtle tonal gradient (5–12% shift from one edge to     the opposite edge). NEVER use a pure flat hex fill. Flat fills read cheap and digital.
  * Every colour field MUST carry a texture overlay: 3–8% noise/grain is the minimum.     Add one of: cream laid paper, linen weave, letterpress emboss, brass foil, film     grain, halftone dots, or risograph misregistration — whichever matches the register.
  * Every typographic element MUST carry texture too: letterpress emboss for Jackson     House (type sits AS IF pressed into cream paper, 2px inner-shadow dark edge + 1px     light highlight); brass foil with subtle specular for Blue Mezcal nocturnal; ink-    bleed / halftone offset for Azteca playful; paper-grain impression for Blue Mezcal     editorial.
  * Photography receives Portra 400 or Portra 800 film-grain emulation (depending on     lighting register) — never a clean digital file.
  * Where a drop shadow is used, follow the pro spec: y-offset 4–8px, blur 16–40px,     opacity 8–20%, colour = a DARKER SHADE of the background (never pure black).     NEVER the default Photoshop 5px-90°-75%-black drop shadow.

LUXURY-GRADIENT EXAMPLES (pick one that fits):
  * Paper-gradient: cream lightens toward the centre, darkens at the edges (letterpress     vignette).
  * Tonal-brand-gradient: cobalt #1E3A8A → #1A3275 in a 5% shift across the field.
  * Foil-gradient: brass #C9A24B → #B08937 → #C9A24B as a soft 3-stop reading like a     foil-stamp highlight catching light.
  * Velvet-gradient: burgundy #4A1621 falling into charred black #0B0B0B at one corner,     like low candlelight.

DROP-SHADOW RULE:
  Only add a drop shadow to type when the palette contrast is INSUFFICIENT on its own   (e.g., cream type over a busy mid-tone photo). When contrast is already strong   (e.g., brass-foil gold on deep forest green, cream on charred black), OMIT drop   shadows entirely — they cheapen the foil-stamp effect. Foil type on a deep tonal   field needs no shadow; the foil itself carries the hierarchy.

FINAL: Render at the aspect ratio specified via the API (not in prose). Dense typography with purpose. Real brand register. No stock gradients, no Canva-template feel. Avoid the AI tells: no cyan-to-magenta gradients, no default Poppins/Montserrat/Open Sans, no 33/33/33 colour balance, no centered dead-symmetric layouts unless ceremonial, no flat-colour fields. Every surface is LAYERED: photo + gradient + noise + emboss / foil / grain.
